title: asclepias incarnata subsp. pulchra
intro: Asclepias incarnata subsp. pulchra, also known as the Butterfly Milkweed, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its vibrant pink or purple flowers that attract various butterfly species. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, derived from its milky sap and leaves. Consuming the plant or using its preparations may offer health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating menstrual cramps due to its estrogenic and antispasmodic effects. Herbal preparations of Asclepias incarnata subsp. pulchra can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, but it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using the plant medicinally.
